author: .paulharris
content: https://github.com/ethereum/beacon-APIs/pull/580 i'd like to try to merge this, <@654267572107083777> <@586161934425128960> <@602753420033785856> <@884836500037066862> thoughts?

author: tersec
content: I won't pretend to like the stateful version, but probably it's going to happen anyway

author: tersec
content: and `builder_boost_factor` was weirdly specified to begin with

author: tersec
content: practically, the 10% or whatever better use cases "never" happen

author: .paulharris
content: shane flagged that boost factor too, but suggested it could be fixed separatly

author: tersec
content: so the practical use cases are never/always

author: tersec
content: how can it be fixed separately?

author: .paulharris
content: put a pr up to fix the issue? https://github.com/ethereum/beacon-APIs/pull/580#issuecomment-4461137358

author: .paulharris
content: i've been avoiding implementing a pending endpoint so its becoming blocking that theres no consensus. sounds like potentially still not consensus

author: tersec
content: ok, the concern is it hits "good enough" and then inertia means it lides into production

author: .paulharris
content: i guess i'll give up on merging it for now, but we need to resolve soemthing here, its needed for gloas to my understanding, and i'd like it resolved so that we can make our VC work

author: tersec
content: I guess, fair disclosure, the merging of this simply isn't critical-path for Nimbus, we're testing with BN-only validators and we can keep doing so

author: tersec
content: Obviously do want to get the API ready

author: .paulharris
content: yeh we're using BN only atm but thats not going to work for mainnet or even testnet

author: tersec
content: sure

author: tersec
content: sepolia/hoodi are the hard deadline here

author: .paulharris
content: theres vc running though, prysm is on the devnet so clearly something is implemented

author: tersec
content: and e.g., for builder_boost, there are now two builder sources which behave in distinct ways

author: tersec
content: should they be merged? probably not because one is fully in-protocol

author: tersec
content: and if it doesn't get on-chain, well, the CL/proposer visibly did its part

author: tersec
content: for the builder API, we'll see

author: .paulharris
content: ok sure - this will just impact delivery because eg. nimbus / teku not implemetning it till late and needing testing etc

author: tersec
content: yeah, we have internal testnet fleets for this

author: tersec
content: as does Teku, I know

author: tersec
content:
the other thing is, a lot of the entities which demand builders actually demand _specific_ whitelisted builders. worth caring about? I'm wary of scope creep but there are at least 3 possible design points for the beacon API to target:

1. status quo, allow both or either
2. drop attempts at requiring builder-only in favor of not-beacon-API-specified mechanisms
3. specify those mechanisms

author: tersec
content: (1) same scope, (2) reduces scope, (3) increases scope

author: .paulharris
content: we should get feedback on that, im not strongly opinionated currently other than trying to avoid going stale due to timeliness concerns

author: tersec
content: basically builder-only is _only there_ to satisfy those entities anyway, AFAICT

author: tersec
content: sure I share those concerns

author: .paulharris
content: probably worth <@412614104222531604> being aware that the block api contines to be blocked

author: tersec
content: like, there's no real reason to actually prohibit local building that I've seen worth caring about except to satisfy these semi-arbitrary external demands

author: tersec
content: it's purely self-sabotage by a node

author: .paulharris
content: id prefer local building is possible at least until we have a firm reason that its not

author: .paulharris
content:
from previous conversations I really can't see https://github.com/ethereum/beacon-APIs/pull/601 ever being accepted for a number of reasons. 
My preference would be that we support ssz on validators like jims v2/validators rather than try to support enums by mapping a value etc.
Interested in feedback on this idea anyway to see what ppl think about adding ssz to validators and validator_balances endpoints
